results.rendered_html: N. of Brahmā (as the creator of the world and disposer of men's fate, sometimes in pl. = prajā-pati, e.g.  Śak. vii, 30/31 ; sometimes Vi-dhātṛ is mentioned together with Dhātṛ, e.g.  MBh. iii, 10419  &c.; both are supposed to be the sons of Brahmā [ MBh. ] or of Bhṛgu [ Pur. ]; in  VarBṛS.  Vidhātṛ is the regent of the 2nd Tithi, while Brahmā presides over the first),  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
vidhātṛ m.
1 A maker, creator;  Ku. 7. 36. 
2 The creator, N. of Brahman; vidhātā bhadraṃ no vitaratu manojñāya vidhaye  Māl. 6. 7 ;  R. 1. 35 , 6. 11 ; 7. 25 .
3 A granter, giver, bestower;  Ku. 1. 57. 
4 Fate, destiny;  H. 1. 40. 
5 N. of Viśvakarman.
6 N. of Kāma, the god of love.
7 Spirituous liquor.
Comp.
— āyus m.
1 sunshine.
2 the sun-flower.
— bhūḥ an epithet of  Nārada.
